---
title: "Haspan Flatsedge (Cyperus haspan)"
slug: "cyperus-haspan"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/474932596/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Haspan Flatsedge"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Liliopsida"
tax_order: "Poales"
tax_family: "Cyperaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Liliopsida", "Poales", "Cyperaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/474932596/medium.jpg" alt="Haspan Flatsedge" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/damien6868">
damien6868</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/264490519">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Liliopsida
-   **Order:** Poales
-   **Family:** Cyperaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=161447)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in moist to wet habitats such as marshes, pond margins, and ditches.

## Natural history notes

A grass-like sedge characterized by its slender, triangular stems and umbrella-like clusters of spikelets at the top. In Florida, it is commonly found in moist to wet habitats such as marshes, pond margins, and ditches. It grows during the warmer months and often occurs in shallow water or saturated soils, contributing to vegetation structure in wetland communities.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/161447-Cyperus-haspan)
-   [Local profile](https://plant-directory.ifas.ufl.edu/plant-directory/cyperus-haspan/)
